body:
This method has a higher computational cost than just "not masking the periphery" (although it may be noted, this cost might be totally negligible compared to the cost of drawing a plot), so may not be desirable.

Discussed on #4519
title: Re-zero immersed fields after creating plotting data

title: Add distributed reductions

body:
This PR fixes some small indexing bugs for `FieldTimeSeries` described in https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/issues/4100

- Changes the way that times are compared in https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/blob/d1e9b46586150f2f3508ac8a861d87ef67e22b1c/src/OutputReaders/field_time_series.jl#L252 and https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/blob/d1e9b46586150f2f3508ac8a861d87ef67e22b1c/src/OutputReaders/set_field_time_series.jl#L10 so that `≈` is replaced with a comparison using a vector norm in the first case, and using an absolute tolerance based on a characteristic timescale in the second case. This avoids returning `false` due to the default relative error criterion when comparing values close to zero. 
- Moves the time array that is indexed in https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/blob/d1e9b46586150f2f3508ac8a861d87ef67e22b1c/src/OutputReaders/set_field_time_series.jl#L25 to the CPU so errors aren't thrown when `fts.times` is a `CuArray`.
title: Fix some `FieldTimeSeries` indexing bugs

body: This PR implements the variance dissipation for a `SplitRungeKutta3Timestepper`. Builds on top of #4522. The output of `validation/implicit_dissipation/variance_dissipation.jl` shows the huge advantage of using an RK3 instead of an AB2 for advection-dominated problems. This is the output

labels.description: So things don't blow up and boil the lobsters alive
labels.name: numerics 🧮

title: RK3 variance dissipation support

body:
This splits the kernel `substep_turbulent_kinetic_energy!` into two separate kernels:

- `substep_turbulent_kinetic_energy_diffusivities!`, which computes `Ke` and `Le`
- `substep_turbulent_kinetic_energy!`, which just computes `e`

This is done to avoid a race condition: computing `Ke` requires use of both `e[i,j,k]` and `e[i,j,k-1]`. The latter can be permuted by another thread in the kernel before it is used for `Ke`.

labels.description: So things don't blow up and boil the lobsters alive
labels.name: numerics 🧮

title: Splitting `substep_turbulent_kinetic_energy!` into two kernels
